quiz.title: Unit 11 Quiz 7, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Arrhenius Theory Review and Brønsted-Lowry Introduction
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Arrhenius Theory, Limitations of Arrhenius Theory, Brønsted-Lowry Theory, Conjugate Acid-Base Pairs, Proton Transfer

questions.question: Which observation supports Arrhenius theory?
questions.explanation: All of these observations support Arrhenius theory. Conductivity shows the presence of ions, sour taste is characteristic of acids, and color changes with indicators are typical acid-base behaviors explained by the theory.

questions.question: A conjugate acid–base pair differs by:
questions.explanation: According to Brønsted-Lowry theory, a conjugate acid-base pair differs by one proton (H⁺). When an acid donates a proton, it becomes its conjugate base. When a base accepts a proton, it becomes its conjugate acid.

questions.question: The Arrhenius theory was proposed in:
questions.explanation: Svante Arrhenius proposed his theory of electrolytic dissociation in 1884 as part of his doctoral thesis, which later earned him the Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1903.

questions.question: Which is NOT explained by Arrhenius theory?
options: Why HCl is an acid in water
Why NH₃ acts as a base in water
questions.explanation: Arrhenius theory is limited to aqueous solutions and cannot explain acid-base reactions in non-aqueous solvents like liquid ammonia. It also cannot explain why NH₃ acts as a base since NH₃ doesn't contain OH⁻.

questions.question: Which is a limitation regarding weak electrolytes?
options: Arrhenius theory cannot explain partial dissociation
Arrhenius theory explains partial dissociation through equilibrium
Arrhenius theory denies existence of weak electrolytes
questions.explanation: Arrhenius theory does explain partial dissociation through equilibrium concepts. Weak electrolytes only partially dissociate, establishing an equilibrium between undissociated molecules and ions.

questions.question: Which modern concept does Arrhenius theory NOT anticipate?
questions.explanation: Arrhenius theory does not include the concept of conjugate acid-base pairs, which was introduced later by Brønsted-Lowry theory. pH, indicators, and conductivity measurements are all consistent with Arrhenius theory.

questions.question: According to Brønsted–Lowry theory, an acid is a substance that:
questions.explanation: According to Brønsted-Lowry theory, an acid is a proton (H⁺) donor. This definition expands beyond Arrhenius to include non-aqueous systems and substances that don't contain H⁺ in their formula but can donate protons in reactions.

questions.question: A Brønsted–Lowry base is a substance that:
questions.explanation: According to Brønsted-Lowry theory, a base is a proton (H⁺) acceptor. This includes substances like NH₃ that don't contain OH⁻ but can accept protons from acids.

questions.question: In the reaction HCl + H₂O → H₃O⁺ + Cl⁻, what is the Brønsted–Lowry base?
questions.explanation: H₂O accepts a proton (H⁺) from HCl to form H₃O⁺. Since it accepts a proton, H₂O is acting as the Brønsted-Lowry base in this reaction.

questions.question: In the reaction HCl + H₂O → H₃O⁺ + Cl⁻, what is the conjugate base?
questions.explanation: HCl donates a proton to become Cl⁻. Therefore, Cl⁻ is the conjugate base of the acid HCl. A conjugate base is what remains after an acid has donated a proton.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the transition from Arrhenius to Brønsted-Lowry theory! 🧪🔬
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the limitations of Arrhenius theory and conjugate pairs.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on proton transfer and identifying acids and bases.
scoreMessages.0: Please review Arrhenius theory, its limitations, and Brønsted-Lowry definitions.
